task_completion.description: Verify that the target document is opened in a word processor and the original text content is preserved.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A GUI word processor (e.g., Calligra Words or LibreOffice) is open displaying 'martinez_msj.odt'. The document contains original keywords such as 'Maria Martinez', 'Pacific Coast Properties', and 'Summary Judgment'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the document text has been changed to a serif font.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text characters have visible serifs (small strokes attached to the ends of letters, like Times New Roman), and the font selection dropdown in the toolbar shows a serif font name.

task_completion.description: Verify that double-spacing has been applied to the document.
task_completion.visual_evidence: There is a wide, visible gap between the lines of text in the body paragraphs, or the line spacing setting in the toolbar/menu is visibly set to 2.0 / Double.

task_completion.description: Verify that the body text is fully justified.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Both the left and right edges of the body paragraphs are perfectly straight and flush with the margins, and the 'Justify' alignment button is highlighted in the toolbar.

task_completion.description: Verify that the H1 sections and H2 subsections are formatted to be visually distinct from the body text.
task_completion.visual_evidence: H1 headings (e.g., 'Introduction', 'Argument') and H2 headings (e.g., 'No Breach of Any Duty') are styled differently from normal text (e.g., bolded, underlined, centered, or using a larger font size).

task_completion.description: Verify that the legal caption at the beginning of the document is properly formatted.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The top section of the document containing the court name ('Superior Court of the State of California'), parties, and document title is structured neatly, typically using a visible or invisible table, or precise tab alignments to create a standard pleading caption layout.

task_completion.description: Verify that the formatted document is saved.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent clicks the 'Save' icon (floppy disk), navigates to File > Save, presses Ctrl+S, or the save icon becomes grayed out indicating there are no unsaved changes.

integrity.description: Check that the agent used a GUI word processor to format the document.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent interacts with a graphical word processor application (like Calligra Words) rather than using terminal commands, python scripts, or a plain text editor to modify the .odt file.

integrity.description: Check that the agent actively applied the formatting rules.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen selecting text and clicking formatting buttons (font, spacing, alignment) or modifying styles in the application menus, rather than just opening an already-formatted file.

integrity.description: Check that the agent did not delete, rewrite, or alter the actual text content of the document.
integrity.visual_evidence: The body paragraphs and legal arguments remain identical to the original text; the agent only changes the visual presentation (spacing, font, alignment, tables for layout).

privileged_info_for_vlm: Document path: /home/ga/Documents/martinez_msj.odt. Expected H1 sections: Introduction, Statement of Undisputed Material Facts, Legal Standard, Argument, Conclusion, Declaration of Service. Expected H2 subsections: Defendant Owed No Duty of Care, No Breach of Any Duty, Plaintiff's Injuries Were Not Caused by Defendant's Conduct, Plaintiff's Comparative Fault Bars Recovery. Content keywords to verify text preservation: Superior Court of the State of California, Pacific Coast Properties, Maria Martinez, Summary Judgment, Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield, Rowland v. Christian, Code Civ. Proc., penalty of perjury.
